title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Richard confesses to his wife, Theo, that he is a thief. To protect himself from potential disgrace and prison, he reveals that Vincent Easton possesses incriminating documents related to his illegal dealings. Richard claims he has meticulously covered his tracks but fears Easton might inadvertently destroy him if he does not realize the connection to the data. Richard pressures Theo to visit Easton immediately to retrieve the files, suggesting that Easton will not refuse her because he likes her. When Theo resists, Richard pulls a revolver from a drawer and threatens to commit suicide if she does not comply. Under this emotional duress, Theo agrees to help.

Theo travels to Easton’s residence wearing a white mink stole. She informs Easton that she is there on behalf of her husband to request the documents. Easton reveals that he has been investigating a company involving names like Hobson, Jekyll, and Lucas, and expresses surprise that Richard is at the top of such a fraudulent operation. Despite the revelation of Richard's character, Easton agrees to provide the files. Theo takes the package and burns the documents in the fireplace.

After the meeting, Theo takes a taxi through Hampstead and toward Chelsea. She experiences internal conflict, doubting herself after seeing Easton, but ultimately decides to remain loyal to Richard, rationalizing that his crimes are against society rather than her. Upon returning home, a trembling Richard asks if everything went well. Theo confirms she burned the documents herself. Exhausted and pale, Theo collapses into an armchair, wishing for eternal sleep, while Richard watches her with a mixture of guilt and embarrassment.
